pr.title: feat: layout-matched skeleton loaders for destination, blog, and detail pages
pr.body:
Generic `CardGridSkeleton` was shared across destinations and blog with wrong image heights and missing structural rows, causing visible layout shift on load. `DestinationDetailSkeleton` was missing the `md:h-[60vh]` hero breakpoint, tab nav, FAQ section, and had an undersized sidebar placeholder.

## Changes

### `src/components/LoadingSkeletons.tsx`
- **`DestinationCardGridSkeleton`** (new) — `h-56 sm:h-52` image, country line, 2-line description, justified footer row (clock · dollar · link); matches `DestCard` exactly
- **`BlogCardGridSkeleton`** (new) — `h-44` image, badge+reading-time+author flex row, title, description, date (`h-3`), "Leer más" placeholder; grid uses `md:grid-cols-2` to match blog's own columns
- **`DestinationDetailSkeleton`** (improved) — hero gains `md:h-[60vh]`, content gains 4-tab nav row, 7 body lines, FAQ accordion placeholder, sidebar bumped to `h-64`
- `CardGridSkeleton` retained for any other consumers

### Consumers updated
- `DestinationsCatalog` → `DestinationCardGridSkeleton`
- `BlogListing` → `BlogCardGridSkeleton count={6}` (was `count={3}`, insufficient to fill a full grid row)
- `DestinationDetail` was already wired to `DestinationDetailSkeleton`
pr.author: Copilot
